faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Harrison home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool, cashmere, or silk garments, silky tubes or cocoons in closet corners, and small tan or gray moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae or webbing on stored clothing, particularly seasonal items kept in closets or attic storage areas common in Harrison homes.

faqs.question: What is the cost of clothes moth control in Harrison?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Harrison typically range from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of affected areas. Our technicians provide free inspections and detailed quotes based on your specific situation before beginning any treatment.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically shows results within 24-48 hours for adult moths. However, complete elimination requires follow-up treatments to address eggs and larvae, usually over a 4-8 week period. We recommend professional cleaning of affected garments and may suggest follow-up inspections to ensure your Harrison home remains moth-free.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage more than just clothing?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths can damage any natural fiber materials including upholstered furniture, area rugs, curtains, and even taxidermy displays. In Harrison's larger homes with extensive furnishings, moths can infest multiple rooms. Our comprehensive treatment protects all vulnerable materials throughout your home, not just your closets.
